Table of Authorized Materiel Control Number (TAMCN):  Alphanumeric set that identifies a piece of equipment within the USMC inventory:1. Employed by the Marine Corps to establish that an item is essential to the operational readiness of a weapons systems. 2. Indicates that a repair part contributes to the tactical and essential operations of an end item, component, or assembly whose failure would make the end item inoperable or incapable of fulfilling its mission. 3. Also used to indicate that a repair or secondary depot reparable component is required for the safety and health of personnel or is required by state or local laws.

The NIIN is the last nine digits of the NSN that differentiates each individual supply item from all other supply items. The first two digits signify the National Codification Bureau that assigned the NIIN, while the last seven digits are non-significant and are sequentially assigned by the FLIS.
